headline: FIRST IN A DECADE: Uranium Energy's Burke Hollow ISR Mine Goes Live in Wyoming, Shares Surge 8.83%
snippet: UEC's Burke Hollow ISR project became the first new US in-situ uranium recovery operation in more than 10 years on April 27, with uranium-bearing solution now being captured and material heading to the Hobson processing plant — all against a licensed capacity of 12.1 million pounds per year.

headline: Perseus Mining Fires First Production Blast at CMA Underground — Côte d'Ivoire's First Modern Underground Mine
snippet: Perseus Mining (ASX/TSX: PRU) completed the first underground stoping blast at its $170M CMA Underground project at Yaouré Gold Mine in Côte d'Ivoire — the first modern underground mine in the country — extending mine life to at least 2035, with planned annual underground mining of 700,000–770,000 tonnes at steady state.

headline: Abcourt Mines Hits 1.7 g/t Gold Over 12 Metres — High-Grade 15.2 g/t Intercept — at Flordin, Quebec
snippet: Abcourt Mines (TSX-V: ABI) completed 11 drill holes totalling 2,960m east of the Cartwright stripping at its Flordin property in Quebec, intersecting 1.7 g/t Au over 12m including 15.2 g/t Au over 1m — part of a 20,000m 2026 campaign now 3,900m complete drilling at a rate of 2,000m per month.

headline: 679 g/t Gold: One Bullion Hits Bonanza Grade in First-Pass Artisanal Sampling at Vumba Project
snippet: One Bullion Ltd. (TSXV: OBUL) identified a 2.5km mineralized corridor at the Vumba Project via first-pass artisanal sampling, returning a headline 679 g/t Au result alongside five additional samples above 10 g/t Au by fire assay — setting up drill targets ahead of a high-resolution airborne geophysical survey.

headline: BCM Resources Drills Deepest Hole in Project History at Thompson Knolls Cu-Au Porphyry — 3,995 Feet
snippet: BCM Resources (TSX-V: B) completed Hole TK15 at the Thompson Knolls copper-gold porphyry-skarn project in Nevada, reaching a record 3,995.5 feet (1,219m) in 38 days to test the down-dip extension of Cu-Au-Ag-Mo skarn mineralization intercepted in the prior Phase 3 campaign.

headline: REPORT: US Mint Buying Gold Sourced from Drug Cartel-Controlled Mines, NYT Investigation Finds
snippet: A New York Times investigation found the US Mint sourcing gold that originated from mines controlled by drug cartels, raising uncomfortable questions about provenance standards in government precious metals procurement — and what it means for the 'responsibly sourced' metal narrative that increasingly underpins ESG-aligned investment strategies.

headline: Solis Minerals Grabs 93,000 Hectares of Rio Tinto's Brazil Lithium Ground for US$500K
snippet: Solis Minerals (ASX:SLM, TSX-V:SLMN) acquired 22 exploration licences covering 93,000 hectares adjacent to major operators in Brazil's Araçuaí–Salinas Lithium Valley from Rio Tinto for US$500,000, with Rio retaining a 1.75% NSR — positioning the dual-listed junior for drill-ready lithium upside in one of South America's most active hard-rock lithium belts.

headline: RARE EARTHS RACE: USAR Surges on Serra Verde, Aclara Delivers US$1.7B NPV Feasibility as Sector Builds Momentum
snippet: Rare earth stocks moved higher on April 27 as investors priced in a structural supply chain shift away from Chinese dominance — USA Rare Earth (USAR) advanced on its Serra Verde acquisition while Aclara Resources (TSX:ARA) gained on completion of its Carina Project feasibility study, which returned an after-tax NPV8 of US$1.7 billion.

headline: GOLD MINERS STILL UNDERVALUED AT $4,700 GOLD: Analyst Sees 'Structural Bull' Catch-Up as Stocks Lag Metal
snippet: Despite gold near $4,700/oz, senior producers trade at just 9.6x next-12-month cash flow versus historical norms — only slightly above the five-year average — as analysts argue that mining equities have yet to fully reprice for the new metal regime; Equinox Gold (EQX) and Alamos Gold are flagged as top catches with specific production catalysts in 2026.
